content.name: web_search
content.input: [object Object]{"query": "Kadavath Saurav \"Language Models Mostly Know What They Know\" arXiv 2207.05221"}

content.text:
Here are the results for your search:

The paper **"Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know"** by Saurav Kadavath et al. is a well-known research paper published on arXiv with the identifier **2207.05221**. Here's what we know about it:
This paper investigates the calibration and self-knowledge capabilities of large language models.

The core finding is that large language models can, to a significant degree, assess whether they know the answer to a given question — a property the authors refer to as "self-knowledge" or calibration.

Key aspects of the paper include:

- **Authors**: Saurav Kadavath and collaborators (from Anthropic)
- **arXiv ID**: 2207.05221
- **Topic**: The paper studies whether language models can evaluate the probability that their own outputs are correct, examining calibration across various question-answering tasks.
- The research explores how models can be prompted to assess their own confidence and whether larger models tend to be better calibrated than smaller ones.

This paper has been influential in the AI safety and alignment community, as understanding when a model "knows what it knows" is important for building trustworthy AI systems. You can find the full paper at [https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.05221](https://arxiv.org/abs/2207.05221).
